front: Short Story: in this story, Carlos Daneri's cellar contains a point in space that contains all other points; the narrator lies on the nineteenth step to see it
Short Story: in this story, the poet Carlos Argentino Daneri writes a poem called 'The Earth' describing every inch of the planet, aided by the title object in his cellar
Short Story: this story imagines a universe of hexagonal rooms containing every possible book; 'Purifiers' search for the 'Crimson Hexagon'
Short Story: in this story, the German spy Yu Tsun shoots the Sinologist Stephen Albert to communicate a city's name; Albert discovered that Ts'ui Pen's novel and labyrinth are one and the same
Short Story: in this story, an encyclopedia describes the idealist world of Tlön with a nounless language; 'mirrors and copulation are abominable'
Short Story: in this story, a fictional French writer recreates Don Quixote word-for-word by seeking to 'be' Cervantes; the narrator considers it superior to the original
back: Pierre Menard, Author of the Quixote (Borges)
work: Pierre Menard, Author of the Quixote

front: Short Story: in this story, the detective Erik Lönnrot investigates murders forming the Tetragrammaton pattern and is killed by Red Scharlach at the fourth point
Short Story: in this story, Ireneo Funes gains perfect memory after falling from a horse, assigning arbitrary names to numbers and unable to generalize
Short Story: in this story, a wizard dreams a man into existence in a ruined temple; the twist reveals the wizard himself was dreamed (he is unharmed by fire)
Short Story: in this story, the narrator trades a black-letter Wycliffe Bible for a book with no beginning or end; he sees an anchor but can never find the same page again

Short Story: this story ends 'I do not know which of us has written this page'; its narrator is partial to 'hourglasses, maps, eighteenth-century typography, the taste of coffee and the prose of Stevenson'
Short Story: in this story, a religious scholar concludes that God incarnated himself as Judas rather than Jesus, since crucifixion doesn't compare to a lifetime of hatred
Short Story: in this story, Emma shoots Aaron Loewenthal and claims rape, avenging her father's suicide caused by Loewenthal's accusations of embezzlement
Author: this author wrote 'I had always imagined Paradise as a kind of library' in his 'Poem of the Gifts'
Author: this author argued in 'Kafka and His Precursors' that writers create their own precursors, claiming Browning's 'Fears and Scruples' prophesies Kafka
Author: this author's tombstone is inscribed with a line from the Old English poem 'The Battle of Maldon'; he taught Anglo-Saxon at the University of Buenos Aires
Author: this author collaborated with Adolfo Bioy Casares under the pseudonym H. Bustos Domecq to write Six Problems for Don Isidro Parodi
Author: this author published Julio Cortázar's 'House Taken Over' in his magazine Los Anales de Buenos Aires
